cluster.title: Body Composition & Health Monitoring — Kefiw
cluster.subhead: Impairment math, chassis density, coolant load, cessation ladder.
cluster.intro: Four instruments for tracking the current physical state: estimated BAC after a drink count, body-fat % from tape measurements, daily hydration requirement, and the nicotine decay timeline after quitting.
cluster.description: Widmark BAC calculator, US Navy body-fat circumference method, hydration requirement by mass and activity, and cotinine cessation timeline.

faq.a: No. Widmark has documented ±30% error and is not admissible anywhere. Only breathalyzer or blood draw is evidence.

faq.q: Why US Navy body-fat, not BMI?
faq.a: BMI cannot tell muscle from fat. Circumference methods validate ±3-4% against DEXA — better for athletic or lean populations.

tools.title: Impairment Coefficient — BAC Calculator (Widmark) | Kefiw

current.description: Blood alcohol estimate via Widmark equation. Gauge shows legal-limit marker and clear-to-operate time. Hard-coded estimate only — not legal evidence.
current.intro: Widmark (1932) remains the forensic standard: BAC = (alcohol_g / (weight_g × r)) × 100 − β × t. r ≈ 0.68 male / 0.55 female, β ≈ 0.015 %/h. Real BAC varies with food, drug interactions, and individual enzyme efficiency.
current.outcomeLine: Widmark BAC gauge with clear-to-operate countdown — estimate only, never legal evidence.
howTo: Enter gender, weight, drinks, and grams alcohol per drink (~14 g = US standard).
Gauge shows current BAC against the 0.08 legal limit; sidebar shows clear-to-operate time.

examples.title: 180 lb male · 3 drinks · 1.5 h
examples.body: BAC ≈ 0.044 · clear in ~3 h. Below 0.08 — not below impairment threshold.

faq.a: No. Widmark-based estimates have well-documented ±30% error and are not legal evidence anywhere. Breathalyzer or blood draw is the only admissible measurement.

faq.q: Why does my BAC feel higher than the estimate?
faq.a: Empty stomach, medication interactions, sleep deprivation, and female physiology (lower ADH) all push real BAC above Widmark.

tools.title: Structural Density — Body Fat Calculator (US Navy) | Kefiw

current.description: Estimate body fat percentage via the US Navy circumference method. Tape-measure formula accurate to ±3-4% vs DEXA.
current.intro: The Navy developed this formula in the 1980s as a field substitute for underwater weighing. Logarithmic regression of neck, waist, and (for women) hip circumferences against height. Validated ±3-4% vs DEXA — better than BMI, worse than a scan.
current.outcomeLine: US Navy body fat from tape-measure circumferences — ±3–4% vs DEXA.
howTo: Measure neck (below larynx), waist (narrowest point), and hip (widest, women only).

examples.title: 70 in · neck 15 · waist 34 · male
examples.body: BF ≈ 17.4% · Fit band. Strip fat by tightening waist − neck delta.

faq.a: The Navy formula models total body volume as head + neck (lean) + trunk + limbs. Waist − neck (men) and waist + hip − neck (women) proxy trunk fat.
Less so. Muscular necks under-count fat; deep-trained cores inflate waist slightly. Accept ±5% for trained populations.

tools.title: Hydraulic Integrity — Hydration Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Daily water requirement anchored to body mass and activity. Coolant-tank visualisation with pressure status.
keywords: how much water should I drink
current.intro: Required fluid ≈ weight × 0.5 oz + activity × 0.4 oz — the simple rule-of-thumb that survives the ACSM literature. Not medical. Kidney-impaired and endurance athletes need individualized targets.
current.outcomeLine: Daily fluid requirement anchored to mass and activity — coolant-tank read.
howTo: Enter weight (lbs), today's active minutes, and current intake (oz).
Tank fills to current intake; colour encodes pressure (green Stable / gold Guarded / magenta Low).
Read the deficit and close it before the next training window.

examples.title: 180 lb · 30 min activity · 32 oz
examples.body: Required ≈ 102 oz · current 32 · Low pressure. Deficit 70 oz.

faq.a: Yes — caffeinated drinks hydrate on net despite being diuretic. Alcohol does not.
No. The "8 glasses" number has no scientific origin. Anchor to body mass and activity instead.

tools.title: Cessation Decay Ladder — Nicotine Clearance | Kefiw

current.description: Plot cotinine decay and system-recovery milestones after the last cigarette. 16-hour half-life; baseline at ~72 hours.
current.intro: Nicotine itself has a 2h half-life; its metabolite cotinine clears at 16h. Detox progress plus CDC-derived system milestones (heart rate, CO, cilia, withdrawal) shows exactly what recovers on what schedule.
current.outcomeLine: Cotinine decay and system-recovery milestones since last cigarette.
howTo: Progress bar shows % baseline; milestones light up as you pass them.
Baseline recovery reads the hours to 95% cleared.

examples.title: 24 hours clean
examples.body: ~64% metabolites cleared · heart rate normalised · CO halved. 48 h away from cilia recovery start.

faq.q: Does vaping have the same timeline?
faq.a: Nicotine pharmacokinetics are identical. Combustion-specific markers (CO, tar) don't apply.
Acute withdrawal peaks days 1–3, resolves by day 14. Situational triggers persist for months.

enhancement_brief.goal: Lift each article from short-form summary to authority-grade long-form. Target 1,200+ words per article while keeping the structured fields crisp.
per_article_outputs: 1. Fill `enhanced` with any structured-field overrides (usually: tighter intro, expanded keyPoints, more examples, richer FAQ).
2. Fill `longform_markdown` with 800-1200 word prose body (markdown, H2 sections only — no H1).
3. Do not modify `current` — that is the baseline snapshot for diffing.

rules: Each question must read like a real search query — not marketing-speak.
First sentence of answer is a complete standalone 15-25 word definition/answer (Google quotes it directly).
Full answer 40-60 words. Tight. Front-load the answer.
Cover distinct intents: definition, comparison, edge-case, how-to, troubleshooting. Not 3 rewordings of the same angle.
Include one target keyword in at least half the questions.
Add a faq_intent tag per entry: "definition" | "comparison" | "edge-case" | "how-to" | "troubleshooting" | "cost" | "trust"
faq_rules.target_count: 5-7 entries per article, 4-6 per tool page

structure: ## H2 section 1 — deeper "what is it" / definition with context
## H2 section 2 — the mechanic / how it works (with one small example)
## H2 section 3 — when to use vs when to avoid
## H2 section 4 — worked example or walkthrough
## H2 section 6 — tool-specific tips (link out to the tool)
longform_rules.tone: Concrete, useful, first-person-plural light. No filler. No "in conclusion". Short paragraphs.
links: Every time you mention a sibling tool in this cluster, link it with the exact URL from this brief (tools[].url).
Every time you mention a sibling article, link it with the exact URL from this brief (articles[].url).
Do not invent URLs or link to external competitors.
forbidden: Medical/legal/financial advice language ("you should", "we recommend") — use neutral framing.
Generic SEO boilerplate ("in today's fast-paced world").

internal_linking.rule: Use only URLs that appear in this JSON. Prefer linking to sibling tools/articles in THIS cluster over outbound links.

quality_gates: No fabricated facts — if uncertain, phrase as "in practice" or remove.
Every H2 section earns its space — cut if it restates the intro.
Zero AI-tells: "it is important to note", "in conclusion", "let us delve", "furthermore".
